Clinical Details:
C/O recurrent carcinoma cervix with anterior abdomen wall deposit, ? Nature
Features are suggestive of Squamous cell carcinoma
Variants Description:
PIK3CA:c.1633G>A:p.Glu545Lys: Pathogenic: The p.Glu545Lys variant (also known as c.1633G>A), was detected in PIK3CA gene
on chromosome 3 at position 178936091 with variant allele frequency of 53.003% (represented by 1059 reads). This heterozygous
mutation is having a total depth of 1998X. It is located at exon 10 of NM_006218.4 transcript and was found to change amino acid,
Glutamic acid to Lysine at codon 545. It leads to Gain-of-Function. It is a hotspot variant. It is represented by rs104886003 in dbSNP
and COSM763 in cosmic database. It is interpreted as pathogenic according ClinVar database [VCV000013655]. It is predicted as
deleterious by SIFT, polyphen2 and Mutation Taster2 which is an in-silico DNA variant effect prediction tool. This variant was found in
the population frequency database like gnomAD exome and ExAC at global minor allele frequency of 0.0004032% and 0.0008340%
receptively.
KMT2D:c.12592C>T:p.Arg4198Ter: Pathogenic: The p.Arg4198Ter variant (also known as c.12592C>T), was detected in KMT2Dgene on chromosome 12 at position 49425896 with variant allele frequency of 35.25% (represented by 685 reads). This heterozygous
mutation is having a total depth of 1943X. It is located at exon 40 of NM_003482.4 transcript and was found to change amino
acid, Arginine to stop codon at 4198. It leads to Loss-of-Function. It is represented by rs587783685 in dbSNP and COSM1512054
in Cosmic database. It is interpreted pathogenic according to ClinVar database [VCV000158722]. It is predicted as deleterious by
MutationTaster2 which is an in-silico DNA variant effect prediction tool. This variant was not found in the population frequency
database like gnomAD, ExAC and 1000G database.
TRIM24(11) - FGFR1(10) Fusion: This fusion was detected between exon 11 of TRIM24 and exon 10 of FGFR1 gene located atgenomic position 138255748 of chromosome 7 and position 38275891 of chromosome 8 respectively. This fusion is supported by 714
reads. This fusion is represented as TRIM24(11) - FGFR1(10). It results in Gain-of-Function.
FGFR1 Fusion is present in 0.18% of AACR GENIE cases, with breast invasive ductal carcinoma, colon adenocarcinoma, prostateadenocarcinoma, invasive breast carcinoma, and lung adenocarcinoma having the greatest prevalence [mycancergenome.org].
Note:Above reported fusion is identified at very low reads, confirmation by other orthogonal method is recommended.
QC statistics: Sample is sequenced at Average base coverage depth of 1,948. The Target base coverage at 500X is 89.26%.
